atoms.semantic_claim: The downstream diff for crates/transaction-pool/src/pool/state.rs introduces behavior or wiring that must be accounted for in the v2.2 merge target.

destinations.evidence: The executable transaction subpool state remains the v2.2 upstream four-pool model; Ritual-specific async/scheduled lifecycle is no longer encoded as SubPool variants.
Async lifecycle state is tracked in a dedicated metadata pool instead of in normal transaction subpool classification.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The old extra SubPool variants are intentionally replaced rather than ported literally. Normal candidate ordering still uses pending/basefee/blob/queued state, while async and scheduled special cases are enforced by async metadata sender locks, canonical commitment handling, payload-built scheduled obligations, and block-verifier checks.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Attempt to add a non-system transaction from a sender with canonical async metadata and expect SenderLocked; canonicalize a commitment and assert the async metadata lock is installed without requiring a SubPool::AsyncCommitment variant.
atoms.notes: Old Scheduled and AsyncCommitment SubPool variants were part of the superseded direct-subpool design. The active v2.2 design keeps async commitments as canonical lifecycle metadata and treats scheduled transactions through payload/verifier state-derived obligations.

summary: Atoms in this file have destination evidence or exact coverage after the feature-ordered pass.
notes: Old Scheduled and AsyncCommitment SubPool variants were part of the superseded direct-subpool design. The active v2.2 design keeps async commitments as canonical lifecycle metadata and treats scheduled transactions through payload/verifier state-derived obligations.
